module-6-name-badge-package.label: Module Project: Build a Name Badge Package
module-6-name-badge-package.summary: Build the welcome-table badge app one helper at a time so it grows from name cleanup into a polished multi-file package.

sketch0.title: A club needs badges before the event starts

mp_3.title: Document and clean the badge helper

mp-1-create-clean-name-module.title: Create the name cleaner module
mp-1-create-clean-name-module.prompt: Start the badge app by moving name cleanup into its own helper file. Create `tools/names.py` with `clean_name(text)`, import it in `main.py`, read one name from input, and print the cleaned result.
mp-1-create-clean-name-module.hint: Put the reusable cleaning logic in `tools/names.py`, then let `main.py` call it.

help.concept: A helper module keeps reusable logic out of `main.py`, which makes the badge app easier to grow.
help.hint_2: In `main.py`, import `clean_name`, read input, and print the cleaned name.

mp-1-create-clean-name-module.starterCode:
raw_name = input()

# Start the badge app by moving name cleanup into tools/names.py.
# Import clean_name, then print the cleaned version of raw_name.

main_py.content:
from tools.names import clean_name

raw_name = input()
print(clean_name(raw_name))

mp-1-create-clean-name-module.solutionCode:
from tools.names import clean_name

raw_name = input()
print(clean_name(raw_name))

mp-2-add-badge-helper.title: Add the badge helper module
mp-2-add-badge-helper.prompt: Keep the working `clean_name` helper from the last step. Now add `tools/badges.py` with `make_badge(name, role)`, import it in `main.py`, read a role too, and print the finished badge.
mp-2-add-badge-helper.hint: Leave `tools/names.py` working, add one new helper file, then connect it in `main.py`.

help.concept: This step adds a second helper file so each module has one clear job: names clean names, badges format badge text.
help.hint_2: In `main.py`, clean the name first, then pass `name` and `role` into `make_badge(...)`.

mp-2-add-badge-helper.starterCode:
from tools.names import clean_name

raw_name = input()
role = input()
name = clean_name(raw_name)

# Keep the name cleaner working, then add tools.badges.
# Import make_badge and print the finished badge for this cleaned name and role.

tools_badges_py.content:
# Add make_badge(name, role) here.
# Return one formatted string for the badge.

main_py.content:
from tools.names import clean_name
from tools.badges import make_badge

raw_name = input()
role = input()
name = clean_name(raw_name)
print(make_badge(name, role))

tools_badges_py.content:
def make_badge(name, role):
    return f"{role.upper()} badge: {name}"

mp-2-add-badge-helper.solutionCode:
from tools.names import clean_name
from tools.badges import make_badge

raw_name = input()
role = input()
name = clean_name(raw_name)
print(make_badge(name, role))

mp-3-document-badge-helper.title: Document and clean the badge helper
mp-3-document-badge-helper.prompt: Start from the working two-helper badge app. Keep the output the same, but improve `tools/badges.py` by adding a docstring and cleaning the role before uppercasing it.
mp-3-document-badge-helper.hint: This step refines the helper in place instead of changing the overall app flow.

help.concept: Small helper functions are easier to maintain when they explain themselves and handle messy input cleanly.
help.hint_1: Add a triple-quoted docstring under `def make_badge(name, role):`.
help.hint_2: Store `role.strip().upper()` in a variable before returning the badge text.

mp-3-document-badge-helper.starterCode:
from tools.names import clean_name
from tools.badges import make_badge

raw_name = input()
role = input()
name = clean_name(raw_name)
print(make_badge(name, role))

# Keep this badge flow working.
# Add the next improvement inside tools/badges.py.

tools_badges_py.content:
def make_badge(name, role):
    # Add a docstring that explains what this helper returns.
    # Then clean role before uppercasing it so extra spaces do not reach the badge.
    return f"{role.upper()} badge: {name}"

main_py.content:
from tools.names import clean_name
from tools.badges import make_badge

raw_name = input()
role = input()
name = clean_name(raw_name)
print(make_badge(name, role))

tools_badges_py.content:
def make_badge(name, role):
    """Return a formatted badge label for a cleaned name and role."""
    clean_role = role.strip().upper()
    return f"{clean_role} badge: {name}"

0.message: Add a docstring to make_badge in tools/badges.py.

1.message: Clean the role before uppercasing it.

mp-3-document-badge-helper.solutionCode:
from tools.names import clean_name
from tools.badges import make_badge

raw_name = input()
role = input()
name = clean_name(raw_name)
print(make_badge(name, role))

mp-4-package-welcome-message.title: Print the final welcome badge
mp-4-package-welcome-message.prompt: Start from the documented badge app. Keep both helpers, store the badge in a variable, and replace the direct badge print with one friendly final line that begins with `Welcome!`.
mp-4-package-welcome-message.hint: Reuse the existing helper flow, but make the final output feel like a polished handoff.

help.concept: The final app uses small helper modules to make the main program read like a simple story: get input, clean it, build the badge, print the welcome message.
help.hint_1: Use `badge = make_badge(name, role)` after cleaning the name.
help.hint_2: Print one line that starts with `Welcome!` and includes the badge text.

mp-4-package-welcome-message.starterCode:
from tools.names import clean_name
from tools.badges import make_badge

raw_name = input()
role = input()
name = clean_name(raw_name)
print(make_badge(name, role))

# Replace the line above with one polished welcome message.
# Store the badge in a variable, then print a line that starts with Welcome!.

tools_badges_py.content:
def make_badge(name, role):
    """Return a formatted badge label for a cleaned name and role."""
    clean_role = role.strip().upper()
    return f"{clean_role} badge: {name}"

main_py.content:
from tools.names import clean_name
from tools.badges import make_badge

raw_name = input()
role = input()
name = clean_name(raw_name)
badge = make_badge(name, role)
print(f"Welcome! {badge}")

tools_badges_py.content:
def make_badge(name, role):
    """Return a formatted badge label for a cleaned name and role."""
    clean_role = role.strip().upper()
    return f"{clean_role} badge: {name}"

0.message: Build the badge with make_badge() instead of hard-coding the final text.

mp-4-package-welcome-message.solutionCode:
from tools.names import clean_name
from tools.badges import make_badge

raw_name = input()
role = input()
name = clean_name(raw_name)
badge = make_badge(name, role)
print(f"Welcome! {badge}")

try-module-6-name-badge-package-sketch0.prompt:
Set up the first helper for the badge app.

1. Create a folder named `tools`.
2. Create `tools/names.py`.
3. In `tools/names.py`, define `clean_name(text)`.
4. In `main.py`, import `clean_name`.
5. Print the cleaned version of `"  ava  "`.

sketch-1.title: A club needs badges before the event starts
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown:
The student welcome club is setting up a check-in table and needs clean, consistent badges before guests arrive. Names come in with extra spaces, roles need consistent formatting, and the app should be easy for another volunteer to maintain.

You will grow the badge tool one step at a time. Each step starts from the previous canonical solution, then adds a new helper, import, docstring, or final output.
